mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 65% of late-cycle periods with falling INDPRO, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside of early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0%, higher borrowing costs immediately pressure equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 returns within the next five trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution remains centered above zero when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the threshold effect disappears in a multivariate regression controlling for inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample tests, or S&P 500 quarterly returns are negative in more than 55% of confirmed tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate revenue expectations, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: Walk-forward testing shows a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 36-month window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in industrial production following a post-shock recovery phase signals rapid capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 60% of post-shock recovery periods where INDPRO exceeds 105, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when inflation exceeds 5%.
